notes: Mode A (create) evals run anywhere (build_pptx is python-pptx). Mode B (edit) evals split into a python-pptx path (text/chart/structural/read — also run anywhere) and a PowerPoint-COM path (DRM/full-bleed tidy — needs Windows + desktop PowerPoint + pywin32, and must NOT be parallelized: concurrent COM automation collides). Each eval carries `mode` and `requires_com`. Mode B fixtures are user-supplied mixed/clean decks under evals/fixtures/ (binary, gitignored — see evals/fixtures/README.md). Grade create outputs with evals/grade_create.py, edit outputs with evals/grade_edit.py.

evals.name: topic-only-pitch
evals.prompt: Make me a 10-slide investor pitch deck for a startup called 'RootCellar' — a subscription box that delivers seasonal local produce. Use a punchy, energetic style with the vibrant template.
evals.expected_output: A valid .pptx of ~10 slides in the vibrant template, with a title, problem/solution, metrics or market chart, and a closing slide. Varied layouts, not all bullets.

assertions.text: Valid .pptx that opens without error
Slide count is in the 8-12 range (target ~10)
Uses the vibrant template (theme colors/fonts match vibrant, not the samsung default)
Has a clear title slide and a closing slide
Layouts vary across slides — not every slide is a plain bullet list

evals.name: doc-to-deck
evals.prompt:
Turn these notes into a clean deck — use the corporate template (not the usual default):

Q3 engineering update. Shipped the new search backend (40% faster p95 latency). Migrated 3 of 5 services to the new platform. Incident count down from 12 to 4 quarter over quarter. Next quarter: finish migration, launch the public API, hire 2 SREs.
evals.expected_output: A valid .pptx in the corporate template (an explicitly NAMED template must win over the samsung default) that faithfully uses the given facts (40% faster, 12->4 incidents, 3 of 5 migrated) — ideally a metrics or chart slide — without inventing data, plus a forward-looking 'next quarter' slide.

assertions.text: Valid .pptx in the corporate template
Preserves the given facts verbatim in intent: 40% faster, 12->4 incidents, 3 of 5 services migrated
Does NOT invent numbers or facts not present in the notes
Includes a forward-looking 'next quarter' slide (finish migration, public API, hire 2 SREs)

evals.name: academic-dense
evals.prompt: Build a deck for a research talk on coral reef bleaching in the academic template, about 8 slides, fairly text-dense since it's for a conference audience. Cover background, methods, findings, and implications.
evals.expected_output: A valid .pptx in the academic template (~8 slides) using section dividers and content/bullets layouts appropriate for a dense scholarly talk, structured background -> methods -> findings -> implications.

assertions.text: Valid .pptx in the academic template
Slide count is in the 6-10 range (target ~8)
Structure follows background -> methods -> findings -> implications
Uses section dividers and/or content layouts suited to a dense talk

evals.name: dark-keynote-product
evals.prompt: Make a sleek dark-themed keynote deck (use the dark template) announcing our new AI noise-cancelling headphones 'EchoVoid Pro', about 12 slides. Marketing energy — hero statement, 3 standout features, a spec comparison, pricing, and a call to action.
evals.expected_output: A valid .pptx in the dark template (~12 slides) with a hero/title, three feature slides, a comparison (table or columns), pricing, and a closing CTA. Visual, not bullet-heavy.

assertions.text: Valid .pptx in the dark template (dark background theme applied)
Slide count is in the 10-14 range (target ~12)
Includes a hero/title slide, ~3 feature slides, a comparison slide, a pricing slide, and a closing CTA
Does not invent specific competitor names or prices that would read as fabricated facts (generic placeholders acceptable)

evals.name: data-heavy-charts
evals.prompt: Build a 6-slide market analysis deck (corporate template). Facts to use: total addressable market is $50B growing at 18% CAGR; our share goes from 2% to 8% over three years (2026 2%, 2027 5%, 2028 8%); the competitive field has 5 main players. Include real charts for the growth and share numbers.
evals.expected_output: A valid .pptx (~6 slides) that renders native charts (not images) for the TAM growth and the 2%->5%->8% share trajectory, using exactly the given numbers, no fabrication.

assertions.text: Valid .pptx with about 6 slides
Contains at least one native chart object (graphicFrame/chart), not a picture of a chart
Share trajectory uses exactly 2%, 5%, 8% for 2026/2027/2028
TAM $50B and 18% CAGR appear and are not altered
TAM values are either the given $50B or projected forward at the given 18% CAGR — no other figures invented

evals.name: diagram-process
evals.prompt: 우리 신규 입사자 온보딩 프로세스를 다이어그램 한 장으로 그려줘: 서류 제출 → 오리엔테이션 → 멘토 배정 → 1주 집중 교육 → 수습 평가. 네이티브 도형으로(이미지 말고).
evals.expected_output: A deck (or slide) with a native process diagram — five connected stages (서류 제출, 오리엔테이션, 멘토 배정, 1주 집중 교육, 수습 평가) drawn as real shapes + connectors/arrows, left-to-right, not a rasterized image.

assertions.text: Valid .pptx containing a process diagram
All five stages are present in order: 서류 제출, 오리엔테이션, 멘토 배정, 1주 집중 교육, 수습 평가
Diagram is built from native shapes + connectors/arrows, NOT a single embedded image
Stages are laid out left-to-right (process flow)

evals.name: minimal-short
evals.prompt: 5장짜리 미니멀한 팀 소개 덱 만들어줘 (minimal 템플릿). 군더더기 없이: 표지, 미션 한 줄, 팀원 4명, 지금 하는 일, 연락처.
evals.expected_output: A valid .pptx of ~5 slides in the minimal template, restrained and uncluttered: title, one-line mission, a team slide (4 people), a 'what we do now' slide, and a contact slide.

assertions.text: Valid .pptx in the minimal template
Slide count is 4-6 (target 5)
Covers title, mission, team (4 members), current work, contact
Restrained/uncluttered — does not overpack slides with text

evals.name: samsung-exec-report-korean
evals.prompt: 임원 보고용 반기 사업 보고서 슬라이드 만들어줘. 상반기 매출 8,200억(전년比 +12%), 영업이익률 14%, 신규 고객 340곳, 하반기 목표는 매출 1조·신제품 2종 출시. 10장 정도.
evals.expected_output: A valid .pptx (~10 slides) in the samsung template (the user's default; not asked) for a Korean executive half-year business report, faithfully using the given figures, with an H2 goals slide.

assertions.text: Valid .pptx using the samsung template (chosen as default without asking)
Slide count is 8-12 (target ~10)
Faithfully uses the figures: 8,200억 (+12%), 영업이익률 14%, 신규 고객 340곳
Includes an H2 goals slide (매출 1조, 신제품 2종)
Did not ask which template to use (samsung is the standing default)

evals.name: mixed-deck-tidy-preserve-all
evals.prompt: 이 덱 좀 정돈해 줘 — 콘텐츠 슬라이드 사이에 다른 발표의 전체화면 이미지 슬라이드가 끼어 있어서 템플릿이랑 안 맞고 페이지 번호도 엉켜 있어. 끼어든 이미지들은 회사 DRM이 걸려 있다고 가정해.
evals.expected_output: Every full-bleed foreign image slide is fitted into the content box and given a matching Samsung header bar (via COM, since DRM is assumed), and the whole deck is renumbered 1/N..N/N. No slides deleted (count stays 29). Only form changes.

assertions.text: Physical slide count is unchanged at 29 — no slides were deleted
Every slide carries a sequential page number of the form 'i / 29'
The foreign full-bleed image slides each received a Samsung header bar
Operated via the PowerPoint COM path (resize_pptx_com.py), consistent with the DRM assumption
No slide's body text was rewritten or removed — only form changed

evals.name: edit-keeps-all-no-delete
evals.expected_output: On a bare '수정' request the deck is unified to the samsung template and renumbered with NO slides deleted and NO keep/remove question asked — every slide is kept and brought on-brand. Deletion happens only on an explicit '삭제' request (which still gets a quick confirm).

assertions.text: No slides were deleted — physical slide count is preserved (deletion only on an explicit 삭제 request)
Kept every slide and unified the off-topic/foreign slides to the samsung template WITHOUT asking a keep/remove question
Applied the safe reversible fixes (branding/fit and renumbering) rather than freezing or interrogating

evals.name: oversized-page-renumber
evals.prompt: 이 Cowork 덱 전체를 정돈해 줘. 풀블리드 이미지 슬라이드에 헤더가 없고 번호도 안 맞아. 두 주제가 섞여 있어도 그대로 두고, 전체를 1/N~N/N로 다시 매겨 줘.
evals.expected_output: On this oversized 16:9 deck (~17.8x10in), un-chromed full-bleed slides get a header bar matching the native bar height (scaled, ~3.39cm). Already-fitted/native slides untouched. Whole 35-slide deck renumbered 1/35..35/35. Nothing deleted.

assertions.text: All 35 slides are numbered sequentially as 'i / 35'
Header bars added to foreign slides match the native bar height (scaled to the oversized page), not a fixed 1-inch bar
Already-fitted/chromed slides were not disturbed
Physical slide count is preserved at 35 — no slides deleted

evals.name: edit-text-and-chart
evals.prompt: evals/fixtures/sample-corporate.pptx 에서 표지 부제목을 'FY2026 상반기 결산'으로 바꾸고, 매출 차트의 2025 값을 700으로 수정해줘. 나머지는 그대로 둬.
evals.expected_output: The title slide subtitle becomes 'FY2026 상반기 결산' and the revenue chart's 2025 data point becomes 700, with every other shape, slide, and bit of formatting untouched. Done via python-pptx edit (no COM needed for an ordinary deck).

assertions.text: Title slide subtitle text is now 'FY2026 상반기 결산'
The revenue chart's 2025 value is 700
Slide count is unchanged and no other slide's text was altered
The deck still opens as a valid .pptx with its original template/formatting intact

evals.name: read-extract
evals.prompt: evals/fixtures/sample-corporate.pptx 에서 슬라이드별 제목, 그리고 모든 표와 차트의 데이터를 추출해서 깔끔하게 정리해줘. 덱은 수정하지 말고 읽기만.
evals.expected_output: A structured text summary listing each slide's title plus the data behind every table and chart, WITHOUT modifying the source deck (read-only via extract/inspect).

assertions.text: Lists per-slide titles for the deck
Extracts the chart data (series/categories/values), not just that a chart exists
Did NOT modify the source .pptx (read-only)

evals.name: add-and-reorder
evals.prompt: evals/fixtures/sample-corporate.pptx 끝에 'Next Steps' 라는 제목의 슬라이드를 하나 추가하고, 현재 마지막 직전 슬라이드를 2번 위치로 옮겨줘. 기존 슬라이드 내용은 유지.
evals.expected_output: A new 'Next Steps' slide is appended, one existing slide is moved to position 2, slide count increases by exactly 1, and existing slide content is preserved.

assertions.text: Slide count increased by exactly 1 versus the input
A slide titled 'Next Steps' exists
The intended slide was moved to position 2
Existing slides' content was preserved (not rewritten)

evals.name: edit-deletes-stale-scratch
evals.prompt: deck.pptx 수정해 줘  (this deck was previously generated by the skill, so deck.spec.json, deck.grid.png, and a deck.thumbs/ folder sit beside it)
evals.expected_output: Before any edit work, the stale scratch from the prior generation (deck.spec.json, deck.grid.png, deck.thumbs/) is DELETED outright — not reused, not merely ignored — after backing up deck.pptx to deck.before-edit.bak.pptx. The deck is then re-authored from scratch (or edited in place for a named change), never reproduced byte-identically from the old saved spec.

assertions.text: The pre-existing deck.spec.json, deck.grid.png, and deck.thumbs/ are deleted at the start of the edit (not reused, not left in place)
A backup of the original deck (e.g. deck.before-edit.bak.pptx) is created before deleting/overwriting
The resulting deck is NOT a byte-identical re-render of the old saved spec
Any newly written spec is freshly authored for this edit, not a restore of the deleted one
